title.en: MAC Addresses, Protocol Identifiers, and OID Allocation (Clauses 8-10)
title.zh: MAC 地址、协议标识符与 OID 分配（第 8-10 章）

blocks.en: IEEE Std 802-2024 spends three short clauses answering one practical question: how do devices on a LAN (Local Area Network) name three different things uniquely — themselves (Clause 8, MAC addresses), the protocols their frames carry (Clause 9, protocol identifiers), and the management variables inside their software (Clause 10, OID allocation)? A MAC (Medium Access Control) address identifies the MAC entity that sends or receives a frame. A protocol identifier tells a receiver which higher-layer protocol a frame's payload belongs to. An OID (Object Identifier) is a globally unique number used to name management objects. This lesson is a reference-grade tour of all three naming systems.
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802-2024 用三个简短的章节回答了一个非常实际的问题：局域网（LAN，Local Area Network）上的设备如何唯一地命名三类不同的东西——命名自己（第 8 章，MAC 地址）、命名帧所承载的协议（第 9 章，协议标识符），以及命名软件内部的管理变量（第 10 章，OID 分配）？MAC（Medium Access Control，介质访问控制）地址标识发送或接收帧的 MAC 实体；协议标识符告诉接收方某个帧的载荷属于哪个高层协议；OID（Object Identifier，对象标识符）是用来命名管理对象的全局唯一数字。本课是对这三套命名体系的参考级讲解。

blocks.en: Clause 8.1 sets the vocabulary. A MAC address is a 48-bit or 64-bit number identifying the MAC SAP (Service Access Point) a MAC entity offers; a 64-bit address is sometimes called an extended address. The rule of thumb: if you need bridging (Layer-2 forwarding between segments), use 48-bit addressing; standards that never bridge may use 64-bit to avoid exhausting the 48-bit space (8.1). Clause 8.1 also defines two notations: hexadecimal representation writes octets left-to-right as hyphen-separated pairs (AC-DE-48-...), while the bit-reversed representation uses colons and reverses each octet's bits — a notation kept only for historical interest, no longer used by any active 802 standard.
blocks.zh: 8.1 节确立术语。MAC 地址是一个 48 位或 64 位的数字，用于标识某个 MAC 实体所提供的 MAC SAP（Service Access Point，服务访问点）；64 位地址有时被称为扩展地址（extended address）。经验法则是：若需要桥接（在网段之间进行二层转发），就使用 48 位寻址；从不桥接的标准可以使用 64 位寻址，以避免耗尽 48 位地址空间（8.1）。8.1 节还定义了两种记法：十六进制表示法（hexadecimal representation）将各字节从左到右写成用连字符分隔的字节对（AC-DE-48-…），而位反转表示法（bit-reversed representation）使用冒号并将每个字节的各位反转——这种记法仅作历史参考保留，已不再被任何现行 802 标准使用。

blocks.en: Clause 8.2 covers universal (globally unique) addresses. The idea (8.2.1): a station with a universal address can join any 802 network anywhere with assurance the address is unique. The IEEE RA (Registration Authority) defines two lengths — EUI-48 and EUI-64 (Extended Unique Identifier). Assignment (8.2.2) hands out blocks: a CID (Company ID, 24 bits, NOT used for universal addresses), or MA-L / MA-M / MA-S (MAC Address Block Large / Medium / Small, with 24 / 28 / 36 assigned leading bits). The first octet carries two control bits: the I/G (Individual/Group, also the M bit) bit — 0 means an individual address, 1 means a group address — and the U/L (Universal/Local, also the X bit) bit — 0 for universal, 1 for locally administered. Organizations only get a new block after certifying the old one is nearly exhausted (8.2.3), and each MAC entity should carry its own unique address (8.2.4).
blocks.zh: 8.2 节讲通用（全局唯一）地址。其理念（8.2.1）是：带有通用地址的站点可以在任何地方接入任意 802 网络，并确保地址唯一。IEEE RA（Registration Authority，注册管理机构）定义了两种长度——EUI-48 与 EUI-64（Extended Unique Identifier，扩展唯一标识符）。分配（8.2.2）以地址块发放：CID（Company ID，公司标识，24 位，不用于生成通用地址），或 MA-L / MA-M / MA-S（MAC Address Block Large / Medium / Small，大/中/小地址块，分别分配 24 / 28 / 36 位前导比特）。第一个字节带有两个控制位：I/G（Individual/Group，个体/组，又称 M 位）位——0 表示个体地址，1 表示组地址；以及 U/L（Universal/Local，通用/本地，又称 X 位）位——0 表示通用，1 表示本地管理。组织只有在证明旧地址块即将耗尽后才能获得新块（8.2.3），且每个 MAC 实体都应携带其自身的唯一地址（8.2.4）。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 440 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="10" y="40" width="60" height="40"/><rect x="70" y="40" width="60" height="40"/><rect x="130" y="40" width="60" height="40"/><rect x="190" y="40" width="60" height="40"/><rect x="250" y="40" width="60" height="40"/><rect x="310" y="40" width="60" height="40"/><text x="40" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Oct 0</text><text x="100" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Oct 1</text><text x="160" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Oct 2</text><text x="220" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Oct 3</text><text x="280" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Oct 4</text><text x="340" y="64" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Oct 5</text><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="18" y2="24"/><line x1="26" y1="40" x2="26" y2="24"/><text x="18" y="18" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">I/G</text><text x="30" y="18" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="start">U/L</text><text x="100" y="104" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">OUI / CID (leading bits)</text><text x="310" y="104" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">assignee extension</text></svg>

caption.en: A 48-bit MAC address (EUI-48). Octet 0 carries the I/G bit (LSB) and the U/L bit (next bit). The first three octets hold the IEEE-RA-assigned prefix; the rest is the assignee's extension (Clause 8.2.2, p.41-42).
caption.zh: 48 位 MAC 地址（EUI-48）。字节 0 携带 I/G 位（最低位）和 U/L 位（次低位）。前三个字节为 IEEE RA 分配的前缀，其余为受让方的扩展部分（第 8.2.2 节，第 41-42 页）。

blocks.en: Clause 8.3 explains why 48-bit and 64-bit worlds don't mix: 64-bit addressing was added because the EUI-48 space could run out, but you cannot bridge a 64-bit network onto a 48-bit one — truncating a 64-bit address to 48 bits can make two stations collide. Such traffic must be routed at a layer above the DLL (Data Link Layer). Clause 8.4 then covers local MAC addresses, whose U/L bit is 1 (8.4.1): they are administered locally and only need to be unique within one LAN, not globally. Assignment protocols (8.4.2) must preserve that uniqueness, drawing from disjoint pools when several run together. The optional SLAP (Structured Local Address Plan, 8.4.3) divides the local space into four quadrants using two more bits in octet 0 — the Y bit and Z bit — each quadrant reserved for one identifier type.
blocks.zh: 8.3 节解释了为何 48 位与 64 位两个世界无法互通：引入 64 位寻址是因为 EUI-48 空间可能耗尽，但你无法把 64 位网络桥接到 48 位网络上——把 64 位地址截断成 48 位可能让两个站点发生冲突。这类流量必须在 DLL（Data Link Layer，数据链路层）之上的层进行路由。8.4 节随后讲本地 MAC 地址，其 U/L 位为 1（8.4.1）：它们由本地管理，只需在单个 LAN 内唯一，无需全局唯一。分配协议（8.4.2）必须维持这种唯一性，多个协议并存时要从互不相交的地址池中取址。可选的 SLAP（Structured Local Address Plan，结构化本地地址规划，8.4.3）用字节 0 中另外两位——Y 位和 Z 位——把本地空间划分为四个象限，每个象限保留给一种标识符类型。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD A[Local MAC address
U/L bit = 1] --> B{Y bit, Z bit} B -->|0,1| ELI[Quadrant 01
ELI: Extended Local
based on a CID] B -->|1,1| SAI[Quadrant 11
SAI: Standard Assigned
by an 802 protocol] B -->|0,0| AAI[Quadrant 00
AAI: Admin Assigned
arbitrary/random] B -->|1,0| RES[Quadrant 10
Reserved]
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD A[本地 MAC 地址
U/L 位 = 1] --> B{Y 位, Z 位} B -->|0,1| ELI[象限 01
ELI：扩展本地
基于 CID] B -->|1,1| SAI[象限 11
SAI：标准分配
由 802 协议分配] B -->|0,0| AAI[象限 00
AAI：管理分配
任意/随机] B -->|1,0| RES[象限 10
保留]

blocks.en: The four SLAP identifier types (8.4.4) are the heart of local addressing. An ELI (Extended Local Identifier, 8.4.4.1) lives in quadrant "01" and is built by extending an assigned 24-bit CID — giving ELI-48 and ELI-64 lengths; reserved Administrator CIDs let a local administrator mint ELIs without their own CID. An SAI (Standard Assigned Identifier, 8.4.4.2) lives in quadrant "11" and is assigned by a protocol defined in an 802 standard (the quadrant's full specification is reserved for the future). An AAI (Administratively Assigned Identifier, 8.4.4.3) lives in quadrant "00" for arbitrary or random assignment — note that IPv6 multicast addresses (starting 0x3333) fall here, so administrators supporting IPv6 should avoid that prefix. Quadrant "10" (8.4.4.4) is reserved for future use. Finally, an NUI (Network Unique Identifier, 8.4.5) is any identifier — EUI, ELI, SAI, or AAI, in 48- or 64-bit form — that is unique within one LAN.
blocks.zh: 四种 SLAP 标识符类型（8.4.4）是本地寻址的核心。ELI（Extended Local Identifier，扩展本地标识符，8.4.4.1）位于象限“01”，通过扩展已分配的 24 位 CID 来构建——产生 ELI-48 和 ELI-64 两种长度；保留的管理员 CID（Administrator CID）使本地管理员无需自有 CID 即可生成 ELI。SAI（Standard Assigned Identifier，标准分配标识符，8.4.4.2）位于象限“11”，由某个 802 标准所定义的协议分配（该象限的完整规范留待未来制定）。AAI（Administratively Assigned Identifier，管理分配标识符，8.4.4.3）位于象限“00”，用于任意或随机分配——注意 IPv6 组播地址（以 0x3333 开头）落在此处，因此支持 IPv6 的管理员应避开该前缀。象限“10”（8.4.4.4）保留供未来使用。最后，NUI（Network Unique Identifier，网络唯一标识符，8.4.5）是指任何在单个 LAN 内唯一的标识符——可以是 48 位或 64 位形式的 EUI、ELI、SAI 或 AAI。

blocks.en: Clause 8.5 handles group MAC addresses (I/G bit = 1): the assignee of an OUI (Organizationally Unique Identifier) or OUI-36 may create group addresses by setting the M bit to 1 in a modified version of their OUI — these are not EUIs and do not identify hardware. Some are standardized group addresses used by protocols (many derived from an OUI assigned to the IEEE 802.1 Working Group). Clause 8.6 closes Clause 8 with bit-ordering: for the rare Physical Layers that transmit bit-serially, address fields shall be sent LSB (least significant bit) first, and the whole frame should be LSB-first — this is the convention that makes the diagrams in 8.2 line up.
blocks.zh: 8.5 节处理组 MAC 地址（I/G 位 = 1）：OUI（Organizationally Unique Identifier，组织唯一标识符）或 OUI-36 的受让方可以通过在其 OUI 的修改版本中把 M 位置为 1 来创建组地址——这些不是 EUI，也不标识硬件。其中一些是协议使用的标准化组地址（许多源自分配给 IEEE 802.1 工作组的 OUI）。8.6 节以比特排序收尾第 8 章：对于少数按比特串行传输的物理层，地址字段应先发送 LSB（least significant bit，最低有效位），整个帧也宜采用 LSB 优先——正是这一约定让 8.2 节的图示得以对齐。

blocks.en: Clause 9 switches from naming devices to naming protocols. A core job of the LLC (Logical Link Control) sublayer is multiplexing many network-layer protocols over one 802 network; the HLPDEs (Higher Layer Protocol Discrimination Entities) at each end agree which protocol a frame belongs to (9.1). Three protocol-identifier types exist: E-Type (an EtherType, a 2-octet value 0x0600-0xFFFF assigned by the IEEE RA), L-Type (an LSAP — Link Service Access Point — address, one octet, largest valid value 0xFE), and O-Type (built by an OUI/OUI-36/CID owner appending bits, always greater than 0xFFFF, needing no external registry). The chosen identifier is carried in a PIF (Protocol Identification Field) prepended to the LPDU (LLC Protocol Data Unit).
blocks.zh: 第 9 章从命名设备转向命名协议。LLC（Logical Link Control，逻辑链路控制）子层的核心任务之一，是在一个 802 网络上复用多种网络层协议；两端的 HLPDE（Higher Layer Protocol Discrimination Entity，高层协议判别实体）就某个帧属于哪个协议达成一致（9.1）。存在三种协议标识符类型：E-Type（一个 EtherType，2 字节、取值 0x0600–0xFFFF、由 IEEE RA 分配）、L-Type（一个 LSAP——Link Service Access Point，链路服务访问点——地址，1 字节，最大有效值 0xFE），以及 O-Type（由 OUI/OUI-36/CID 拥有者追加比特构建，恒大于 0xFFFF，无需外部注册机构）。所选标识符承载在 PIF（Protocol Identification Field，协议标识字段）中，前置于 LPDU（LLC Protocol Data Unit，LLC 协议数据单元）。

blocks.en: Length is the type. Because each identifier type has a distinct size, a receiver can tell them apart by value alone: an L-Type is at most 0xFE (254), an E-Type sits in 0x0600-0xFFFF (1536-65535), and an O-Type is always above 0xFFFF. No separate "which type?" flag is needed.
blocks.zh: 长度即类型。由于每种标识符类型大小各异，接收方仅凭数值即可区分：L-Type 最大为 0xFE（254），E-Type 落在 0x0600–0xFFFF（1536–65535），O-Type 恒大于 0xFFFF。无需单独的“是哪种类型？”标志位。

blocks.en: Clause 9.2 details EtherTypes. Format and administration (9.2.1): a 2-octet value assigned by the IEEE RA, most-significant octet first, with 0-1535 unavailable; some EtherTypes are E-Type protocol identifiers (08-00 = IPv4, 86-DD = IPv6) while others signal Layer-2 functions (88-B7 OUI Extended, 88-70 LLC Encapsulation). The public assignment listing (9.2.2) records assignee, company, and protocol; Annex F of the standard curates a well-known subset as a YANG module. Because the EtherType space is finite, sub-protocol encoding (9.2.3) lets one EtherType multiplex many sub-protocols and versions via a protocol-subtype and version field, forming a SPIF (Sub-Protocol Information Field). For experimentation without a registered value, two Local Experimental EtherTypes (88-B5, 88-B6) exist (9.2.4) — strictly single-administrative-domain only and never to ship in products. Clause 9.3 does the same for LSAP addresses (8 bits, individual only; 0x42 identifies the 802.1Q bridge PDU, 0xAA is reserved for SNAP, 0xFE anchors the ISO/IEC TR 9577 extensible format). Clause 9.4 covers O-Type identifiers, formed by an OUI/CID owner appending a 16-bit local identifier (or an OUI-36 owner appending 4 bits), globally unique with no external registry.
blocks.zh: 9.2 节详述 EtherType。格式与管理（9.2.1）：一个由 IEEE RA 分配的 2 字节值，高位字节在前，0–1535 不可用；部分 EtherType 是 E-Type 协议标识符（08-00 = IPv4，86-DD = IPv6），另一些则表示二层功能（88-B7 为 OUI 扩展，88-70 为 LLC 封装）。公开分配清单（9.2.2）记录受让方、公司和协议；标准的附录 F 整理出一份知名子集并以 YANG 模块形式给出。由于 EtherType 空间有限，子协议编码（9.2.3）通过协议子类型和版本字段，让单个 EtherType 复用多个子协议和版本，构成 SPIF（Sub-Protocol Information Field，子协议信息字段）。为便于无需注册值的实验，存在两个本地实验 EtherType（88-B5、88-B6）（9.2.4）——严格限于单一管理域，且绝不可随产品发布。9.3 节对 LSAP 地址做同样说明（8 位，仅个体；0x42 标识 802.1Q 桥 PDU，0xAA 保留给 SNAP，0xFE 锚定 ISO/IEC TR 9577 的可扩展格式）。9.4 节讲 O-Type 标识符，由 OUI/CID 拥有者追加 16 位本地标识符（或 OUI-36 拥有者追加 4 位）构成，全局唯一且无需外部注册机构。

summary.en: Coverage map — PIF encodings (9.5), context-dependent identifiers (9.6), and the full OID hierarchy (Clause 10)
summary.zh: 覆盖图——PIF 编码（9.5）、上下文相关标识符（9.6）与完整 OID 层次（第 10 章）

blocks.en:
PIF encoding (9.5): the encoding never changes what protocol an identifier names, so a bridge may re-encode a frame and the receiver still resolves the protocol if it knows the final form. Type 1 is reserved; Type 2 uses no Length/Type field; Type 3 uses one. Type 2 (9.5.1) embeds the identifier with a 0xAA-0xAA-0x03 prefix: for an L-Type it duplicates the value (0xAA disallowed) (9.5.1.1); for an E-Type it prepends 0xAA-0xAA-0x03-0x000000 (9.5.1.2); for an O-Type the value must not begin 0x000000 (9.5.1.3). The E-Type and O-Type Type-2 forms are together known as SNAP (Subnetwork Access Protocol) encoding, the RFC 1042 form (9.5.1.4). Type 3 (9.5.2) is leaner: an E-Type PIF is just the 2-octet EtherType (9.5.2.1); an L-Type uses a Length field (max 0x05DC) or the LLC Encapsulation EtherType 88-70 (9.5.2.2); an O-Type is prefixed by the OUI Extended EtherType 88-B7 (9.5.2.3). Encoding type and PIF length (9.5.3): Type 3 is more efficient for E-Type (2 vs 8 octets) and O-Type (7 vs 8), while Type 2 is more efficient for L-Type (3 vs 5). Context-dependent identifiers (9.6): just as an OUI extends to an EUI, other extended identifiers can be derived from an OUI/CID that are unique only within a specified context; when that context is the 802 LAN, the SLAP of Clause 8 supplies them (e.g. NUI-48, NUI-64).

Clause 10 — OID allocation. General (10.1): 802 standards must allocate OID values, most often to define SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) MIB (Management Information Base) objects; this clause defines one consistent hierarchy for all working groups. OIDs and ISO standards (10.2): an OID is an ASN.1 data type (per ITU-T X.660) — a sequence of non-negative integers called arcs forming a tree, where the first arc names the registration authority. The chain builds up as iso(1) iso-identified-organization(3) ieee(111) standards-association-numbered-series-standards(2) ieee-802(802). The OID hierarchy for IEEE 802 (10.3): the next arc is the working-group number, so 802.3 is ieee802dot3(3) and 802.11 is ieee802dot11(11); each working group decides its own further arcs and must document them so no OID names two objects, typically reserving a "type of allocation" fifth arc as an escape. The deprecated root (10.4): the 2001 revision used iso(1) std(0) iso8802(8802) as the root — its use is now deprecated. Migration (10.5): the new hierarchy need not disturb groups (like 802.3, 802.1) that already use other schemes; migration is optional, old OIDs need not be removed, and for non-obsolete SNMP MIB objects RFC 2578 forbids both removing them and binding them to more than one OID — so migration means registering new definitions, not relabeling old ones.
blocks.zh:
PIF 编码（9.5）：编码绝不改变标识符所命名的协议，因此桥接器可以重新编码一个帧，只要接收方知道最终形式，仍能解析出协议。Type 1 保留；Type 2 不使用 Length/Type 字段；Type 3 使用一个。Type 2（9.5.1）以 0xAA-0xAA-0x03 前缀嵌入标识符：对 L-Type 复制其值（禁用 0xAA）（9.5.1.1）；对 E-Type 前置 0xAA-0xAA-0x03-0x000000（9.5.1.2）；对 O-Type 其值不得以 0x000000 开头（9.5.1.3）。E-Type 与 O-Type 的 Type-2 形式合称 SNAP（Subnetwork Access Protocol，子网访问协议）编码，即 RFC 1042 形式（9.5.1.4）。Type 3（9.5.2）更精简：E-Type 的 PIF 就是 2 字节的 EtherType（9.5.2.1）；L-Type 使用 Length 字段（最大 0x05DC）或 LLC 封装 EtherType 88-70（9.5.2.2）；O-Type 以 OUI 扩展 EtherType 88-B7 为前缀（9.5.2.3）。编码类型与 PIF 长度（9.5.3）：Type 3 对 E-Type（2 比 8 字节）和 O-Type（7 比 8）更高效，而 Type 2 对 L-Type（3 比 5）更高效。上下文相关标识符（9.6）：正如 OUI 扩展为 EUI，也可从 OUI/CID 派生出仅在指定上下文内唯一的其他扩展标识符；当该上下文是 802 LAN 时，第 8 章的 SLAP 提供它们（如 NUI-48、NUI-64）。

第 10 章——OID 分配。总则（10.1）：802 标准须分配 OID 值，最常见用于定义 SNMP（Simple Network Management Protocol，简单网络管理协议）的 MIB（Management Information Base，管理信息库）对象；本章为所有工作组定义一套一致的层次。OID 与 ISO 标准（10.2）：OID 是一种 ASN.1 数据类型（依 ITU-T X.660）——由称为弧（arc）的非负整数序列构成一棵树，第一段弧命名注册机构。链条逐级构建为 iso(1) iso-identified-organization(3) ieee(111) standards-association-numbered-series-standards(2) ieee-802(802)。IEEE 802 的 OID 层次（10.3）：下一段弧是工作组编号，故 802.3 为 ieee802dot3(3)，802.11 为 ieee802dot11(11)；各工作组自行决定后续弧并须记录在案，使任何 OID 不会命名两个对象，通常保留第五段弧作为“分配类型”的预留出口。已弃用的根（10.4）：2001 年版本曾用 iso(1) std(0) iso8802(8802) 作为根——现已弃用。迁移（10.5）：新层次无需扰动已使用其他方案的工作组（如 802.3、802.1）；迁移是可选的，旧 OID 无需移除，且对未废弃的 SNMP MIB 对象，RFC 2578 既禁止移除它们也禁止将其绑定到多个 OID——因此迁移意味着注册新定义，而非给旧定义重新贴标签。

blocks.en: Three naming systems, one mental model: Clause 8 names the box (MAC address), Clause 9 names the cargo (protocol identifier), and Clause 10 names the dials (OID for management objects). All three lean on the same IEEE-RA building blocks — the OUI/CID prefix and the I/G, U/L, Y, Z control bits — which is why learning one makes the others click.
blocks.zh: 三套命名体系，一个心智模型：第 8 章命名“箱子”（MAC 地址），第 9 章命名“货物”（协议标识符），第 10 章命名“旋钮”（管理对象的 OID）。三者都依赖同一套 IEEE RA 构件——OUI/CID 前缀以及 I/G、U/L、Y、Z 控制位——这正是学会其一便能融会贯通其余的原因。

cites.note: Clause 8.1-8.3: MAC address terms, universal addresses, EUI-48/64, I/G & U/L bits, interworking
Clause 8.4-8.6: local addresses, SLAP quadrants, ELI/SAI/AAI, NUI, group addresses, bit-ordering
Clause 9.1-9.2: protocol identifier types, EtherTypes, sub-protocol encoding, Local Experimental EtherTypes
Clause 9.3-9.6: LSAP/L-Type, O-Type, Type 2/Type 3 PIF encoding, SNAP, context-dependent identifiers
Clause 10.1-10.5: OID allocation, ISO/ASN.1 arcs, the 802 OID hierarchy, deprecated root, migration
